iitjammu.ac.in__cif.md.q: What is the name of the Central Instrumentation Facility at IIT Jammu?

iitjammu.ac.in__cif.md.a: The Central Instrumentation Facility (Saptarshi Labs) is located at the Paloura campus of IIT Jammu.

iitjammu.ac.in__cif.md.q: What type of research does the SAPTARSHI facility support?

iitjammu.ac.in__post__nitinext.md.q: What is NitiNext – The Youth Dialogue?
iitjammu.ac.in__post__nitinext.md.a: NitiNext – The Youth Dialogue is a 3-Day Model United Nations (MUN) conference for school students.

iitjammu.ac.in__post__nitinext.md.q: Who is organizing NitiNext – The Youth Dialogue?
iitjammu.ac.in__post__nitinext.md.a: NitiNext – The Youth Dialogue is organized by the Indian Institute of Technology Jammu in collaboration with I3C-IIT Jammu and NurtureNext (Techible).

iitjammu.ac.in__post__nitinext.md.q: What is the target audience for NitiNext – The Youth Dialogue?
iitjammu.ac.in__post__nitinext.md.a: The target audience for NitiNext – The Youth Dialogue is school students in Grades 7–12.

iitjammu.ac.in__post__nitinext.md.q: When is NitiNext – The Youth Dialogue scheduled to take place?
iitjammu.ac.in__post__nitinext.md.a: NitiNext – The Youth Dialogue is scheduled to take place from 8 - 10 May 2026.

iitjammu.ac.in__post__nitinext.md.q: Where will NitiNext – The Youth Dialogue be held?
iitjammu.ac.in__post__nitinext.md.a: NitiNext – The Youth Dialogue will be held at the Indian Institute of Technology Jammu.

iitjammu.ac.in__post__nitinext.md.q: What are the event highlights of NitiNext – The Youth Dialogue?
iitjammu.ac.in__post__nitinext.md.a: Event highlights include a 3-Day Youth Policy & Diplomacy Conclave, 6+ Diplomacy Committees for debates and discussions, Communication & Diplomacy Masterclasses by IIT Jammu Faculty, a National Platform for School Students (Grades 7–12), and Certification by IIT Jammu.

iitjammu.ac.in__post__nitinext.md.q: What is the last date to apply for School Ambassadors?
iitjammu.ac.in__post__nitinext.md.a: The last date to apply for School Ambassadors is 17 March 2026.

iitjammu.ac.in__post__nitinext.md.q: When do delegate registrations for NitiNext – The Youth Dialogue open?
iitjammu.ac.in__post__nitinext.md.a: Delegate registrations for NitiNext – The Youth Dialogue open from 20 March - 19 April 2026.

iitjammu.ac.in__post__nitinext.md.q: Where can School Ambassadors register for NitiNext – The Youth Dialogue?
iitjammu.ac.in__post__nitinext.md.a: School Ambassadors can register at https://nurturenext.io.

iitjammu.ac.in__post__nitinext.md.q: What is the purpose of NitiNext – The Youth Dialogue?
iitjammu.ac.in__post__nitinext.md.a: The purpose of NitiNext – The Youth Dialogue is to provide a national platform for young learners to engage in policy discussions, diplomacy simulations, and structured dialogue on contemporary global issues while developing critical leadership and communication skills.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__academics__post__doc.q: What is the purpose of the online educational certificate verification portal at IIT Jammu?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__academics__post__doc.a: The purpose is to verify the educational certificates of students who have been conferred degrees from IIT Jammu.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__academics__post__doc.q: Is there a fee for using the online educational certificate verification portal?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__academics__post__doc.a: Yes, a nominal fee has to be paid by the third party during the verification process.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__academics__post__doc.q: What options are available for verifying documents online?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__academics__post__doc.a: The options available are: (a) Mobile Applications available on Play store & Apple Store, and (b) Web Based Application.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__academics__post__doc.q: What is the first step to verify documents using the online portal?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__academics__post__doc.a: The first step is for the verifier to register on the app by creating an account free of cost by signing up as a normal user.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__academics__post__doc.q: What is the second step to verify documents using the online portal?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__academics__post__doc.a: The second step is for the verifier to scan the QR scan code present in the document to verify the documents.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__academics__post__doc.q: Where can the mobile applications for document verification be found?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__academics__post__doc.a: The mobile applications can be found on the Play store and Apple Store.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__academics__post__doc.q: What is the link for the web-based application mentioned in the document?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__academics__post__doc.a: The link for the web-based application is not provided in the document.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__academics__post__doc.q: Is the account creation for verifiers on the online portal free of cost?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__academics__post__doc.a: Yes, the account creation for verifiers on the online portal is free of cost.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__academics__post__doc.q: What is required to verify documents using the online portal?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__academics__post__doc.a: To verify documents using the online portal, verifiers need to scan the QR scan code present in the document.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__academics__post__doc.q: Who needs to pay the nominal fee for document verification?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__academics__post__doc.a: The third party needs to pay the nominal fee for document verification.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__chemical-engineering.q: What is the role of a chemical engineer?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__chemical-engineering.a: A chemical engineer is responsible for converting raw materials into finished products, such as petrol from crude oil or synthetic rubber from rubber sap.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__chemical-engineering.q: What are the 'BIG FOUR' disciplines of Engineering Science?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__chemical-engineering.a: The 'BIG FOUR' disciplines of Engineering Science are Civil, Mechanical, Electrical, and Chemical Engineering.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__chemical-engineering.q: What is the origin of Chemical Engineering?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__chemical-engineering.a: Chemical Engineering has a humble origin in 1888, under the vision of Gorge E Davis.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__chemical-engineering.q: What are some areas where a chemical engineer can work?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__chemical-engineering.a: A chemical engineer can work in areas such as petroleum or petrochemical, polymers, water and wastewater, fine/bulk chemicals, pharmaceuticals, natural products, pulp and paper, food and beverages, nuclear energy, biomedical devices, paints and dyes.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__chemical-engineering.q: What are some global challenges that chemical engineers are called to solve?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__chemical-engineering.a: Chemical engineers are called to solve challenges such as air pollution, valorization of solid waste, water pollution, power generation, medical devices, and infrastructure.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__chemical-engineering.q: How has the focus of Chemical Engineering changed over the last century?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__chemical-engineering.a: Chemical Engineering has moved from its traditional forte of Oil and Gas to Pharmaceuticals and Biological production.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__chemical-engineering.q: What are the educational backgrounds of chemical engineers?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__chemical-engineering.a: Chemical engineers are trained in physics, chemistry, mathematics, economics, biological sciences, and environmental sciences.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__chemical-engineering.q: Which companies are examples of chemical companies?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__chemical-engineering.a: Three of the top ten Fortune 500 companies are chemical companies.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__chemical-engineering.q: What is the financial growth potential for a chemical engineer?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__chemical-engineering.a: A chemical engineer grows higher financially as they gain experience, in majority of the cases the growth is exponential.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__chemical-engineering.q: Who is the visionary behind the origin of Chemical Engineering?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__chemical-engineering.a: Gorge E Davis is the visionary behind the origin of Chemical Engineering.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__chemical-engineering.q: What is the role of a chemical engineer in converting raw materials to finished products?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__chemical-engineering.a: A chemical engineer is responsible for converting any raw material to a finished product, such as petrol from crude oil, synthetic rubber from rubber sap, tea bags from tea leaves, and sugar from sugarcane.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__cif.md.q: How many labs are part of the SAPTARSHI facility at IIT Jammu?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__cif.md.a: The SAPTARSHI facility at IIT Jammu comprises seven labs.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__cif.md.q: What are the names of the labs under the SAPTARSHI facility at IIT Jammu?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__cif.md.a: The labs under the SAPTARSHI facility are: Microscopy Lab, Spectroscopy Lab, X-ray Based Characterization Lab, Optics and Chromatography Lab, Nanofabrication and Physical Property Characterization Lab (NPPCL), Thermal Analysis Lab (TAL), and Water Quality Testing Lab (WQTL).

www.iitjammu.ac.in__cif.md.a: The purpose of the Central Instrumentation Facility (Saptarshi Labs) at IIT Jammu is to enable researchers to explore unexplored domains of basic sciences and engineering.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__cif.md.q: What type of research does the SAPTARSHI facility support?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__cif.md.a: The SAPTARSHI facility supports basic, applied, and medical sciences research.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__cif.md.q: Where is the Central Instrumentation Facility (Saptarshi Labs) located at IIT Jammu?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__cif.md.a: The Central Instrumentation Facility (Saptarshi Labs) is located at the Paloura campus of IIT Jammu.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__cif.md.q: What is the goal of the research conducted at the SAPTARSHI facility?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__cif.md.a: The goal is to publish research findings in peer-reviewed high-impact factor journals for a sustainable society.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__cif.md.q: What type of instruments are available at the SAPTARSHI facility?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__cif.md.a: The SAPTARSHI facility is equipped with highly sophisticated state-of-the-art instruments.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__cif.md.q: Who benefits from the research conducted at the SAPTARSHI facility?

www.iitjammu.ac.in__civil_engineering__p.q: What is the focus of the Structural Engineering division at IIT Jammu?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__civil_engineering__p.a: The Structural Engineering division is focused on creation of new materials tailored for different uses, conventional and new methods for infrastructure design, innovative construction techniques, developing techniques for health monitoring of existing and new structures, developing methods for strengthening aged infrastructure, and smart infrastructure for natural and built environment.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__civil_engineering__p.q: What types of infrastructure does the Structural Engineering division work on?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__civil_engineering__p.a: The infrastructure part includes buildings, tunnels, bridges, and other built environment structures.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__civil_engineering__p.q: What are some of the research areas of the Structural Engineering division?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__civil_engineering__p.a: Research areas include Behaviour of quasi-brittle materials under non-uniform passive and active confinement, RC structures under monotonic and cyclic loading, Prestressed concrete, Fatigue and Fracture, Concrete wind turbine towers, Damage assessment in existing structures, Durability of RC structures, Concrete Damage Plasticity, Fatigue behaviour of polymer composites, Fluid flow modelling in fracturing porous materials, Fracture behaviour of polymer composites, FRP strengthening, Homogenization of composite materials and multiscale modelling, Impact testing, Application of Machine Learning, Meta structures, Microstructure analysis, Multi-scale multi-phase modelling of Geomaterial, Non-destructive Evaluation of Concrete Structures using Ultrasound, Numerical modelling of elastic wave propagation and scattering, Polymer composites, Seismic analysis of RCC structures, Spectral element method (SEM), Subsurface defect detection and characterization, Subsurface imaging, Subsurface imaging and characterization, Surrogate modelling, Ultra high performance concrete (UHPC), Ultrasonic Non-destructive evaluation, Wave Propagation, Wear behaviour of polymer composites, Wind flow in complex terrains, Wind-structure interaction, and Wind-tunnel modelling.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__civil_engineering__p.q: Does the Structural Engineering division work on tunnel systems?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__civil_engineering__p.a: Yes, the Structural Engineering division closely works on the structural part of the Tunnel systems and collaboratively deals with ongoing tunnelling projects at different parts of J&K region.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__civil_engineering__p.q: What is the ultimate goal of the Structural Engineering division's work?

www.iitjammu.ac.in__computer_science_eng.q: Who is the Assistant Professor in the Computer Science & Engineering department at IIT Jammu?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__computer_science_eng.a: Gaurav Varshney

www.iitjammu.ac.in__computer_science_eng.q: What is Gaurav Varshney's research experience?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__computer_science_eng.a: Visiting Researcher, National Institute of Informatics, NII, Japan, December 2019 Research and Technical Assistant (Dec 2017 – Dec 2018), project entitled “Privacy Enhancing Revocable Biometric Entities (PERBI)” sponsored by Board of Research in Nuclear Sciences, Department of Atomic Energy, Government of India.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__computer_science_eng.q: Who is the Professor (Director) in the Computer Science & Engineering department at IIT Jammu?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__computer_science_eng.a: Manoj Singh Gaur

www.iitjammu.ac.in__computer_science_eng.q: What is Manoj Singh Gaur's research experience?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__computer_science_eng.a: Prof. Gaur has successfully completed a number of funded research projects from various funding agencies like MEiTY, DST, UKIERI, CEFIPRA. He has extensive international collaborations with Universities in USA, UK, Spain, Germany, France, Italy, Russia, Australia, New Zealand and Japan. He has been instrumental in establishing Information Security Group and also initiating M.Tech programs, one in Information Security, in MNIT Jaipur. He has also handled a number of Institutional projects. He has supervised seventeen PhDs till date and currently, ten research scholars, under-graduates and post-graduates are working actively in his research group. Prof. Gaur has published extensively in various peer reviewed journals and conferences and is part of a number of conferences of his research domain.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__computer_science_eng.q: Who is the Assistant Professor in the Computer Science & Engineering department at IIT Jammu with research interests in speech signal processing?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__computer_science_eng.a: Mrinmoy Bhattacharjee

www.iitjammu.ac.in__computer_science_eng.q: What is Mrinmoy Bhattacharjee's research experience?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__computer_science_eng.a: Dr. Bhattacharjee has worked as a Research Scientist at the LEAP Lab, Indian Institute of Science Bangalore from June 2024 to November 2024. His work focussed on developing Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) systems for multi-lingual, multi-talker, code-mixing and code-switching conversations in Indian languages. Prior to that, he worked as a Postdoctoral Researcher at the IDIAP Research Institute in Switzerland from May 2023 to May 2024. He worked on developing ASR solutions for Air Traffic Control communications with particular focus on improving recognition of rare domain specific words. He was also engaged as a Senior Project Engineer at IIT Dharwad in a MeitY funded project under the National Language Translation Mission of the Govt. of India. The project goal was to build speaker recognition systems for Indian languages. Dr. Bhattacharjee has also worked as an Assistant Project Engineer in a DST, Govt. of India sponsored project at IIT Guwahati that aimed at developing online handwriting recognition systems for the Assamese language. Dr. Bhattacharjee completed his Ph.D. in Signal Processing & Machine Learning from IIT Guwahati, under the supervision of Prof. S. R. Mahadeva Prasanna and Dr. Prithwijit Guha. He completed his M.Tech. in Computer Technology from NIT Raipur abd B.Tech. in Information Technology from Assam (Central) University Silchar.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__computer_science_eng.q: What are the research interests of Mrinmoy Bhattacharjee?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__computer_science_eng.a: Speech signal processing, machine/deep learning, audio classification/detection, speaker verification, dialect/language identification and vocal mode classification.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__computer_science_eng.q: Who is the Assistant Professor in the Computer Science & Engineering department at IIT Jammu with research experience at IISc Bangalore?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__computer_science_eng.a: Samaresh Bera

www.iitjammu.ac.in__computer_science_eng.q: What is Samaresh Bera's research experience?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__computer_science_eng.a: Assistant Professor (2023): CV Raman Global University, Bhubaneswar, India Teaching Assistant Professor (2022-2023): Villanova University, Philadelphia, USA Postdoctoral Fellow (2019-2022): Virginia Modeling, Analysis and Simulation Center, Virginia, USA. Adjunct Assistant Professor (2019 -2022 ): Old Dominion University, Virginia, USA. Assistant Professor (2018-2019): Manipal University Jaipur, India. Postdoctoral Fellow (2016-2018): University of Padova, Italy. Junior Research Associate (2015-2016): IISc, Bangalore

www.iitjammu.ac.in__ee__research-areas.h.q: What are the research areas in Electrical Engineering at IIT Jammu?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__ee__research-areas.h.a: Microelectronics & VLSI Design, Power System, Communication Systems, RF and Microwave, Signal and Image Processing, Power Electronics

www.iitjammu.ac.in__ee__research-areas.h.q: What are some specific research topics under Microelectronics & VLSI Design?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__ee__research-areas.h.a: Compound Semiconductor based emitter and detector, Low Power High Performance circuit designs, Soft error tolerant circuits, VLSI Testing and Hardware Security, Reliability Modeling of Semiconductor Devices

www.iitjammu.ac.in__ee__research-areas.h.q: Which research topics fall under Power System?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__ee__research-areas.h.a: Robust and Efficient System Operation and Planning, Renewable integration in power system, Operation and Control of Microgrid

www.iitjammu.ac.in__ee__research-areas.h.q: What are the research areas in Communication Systems?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__ee__research-areas.h.a: Smart Metering, Powerline Communication and IoT, Cognitive Radio, Physical Layer Security, and FSO, NOMA, Molecular Communication, and THz Communication, UAV Based Communication and 5G Architecture, Wireless Powered Communication

www.iitjammu.ac.in__ee__research-areas.h.q: What are some research topics in RF and Microwave?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__ee__research-areas.h.a: Microwave and Millimeter Wave Polarization Converters, Microwave Absorbers and Cloaking, Microwave and Millimeter Wave Antennas for 5G, Metamaterials, Microsatellite Antenna Design and Computational EM, Drone Detection and Anti Drone Systems

www.iitjammu.ac.in__ee__research-areas.h.q: What are the research areas in Signal and Image Processing?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__ee__research-areas.h.a: Underwater Signal Processing, Image and Video Processing, Speech and Audio Signal Processing, Machine Learning, Medical Instrumentation

www.iitjammu.ac.in__ee__research-areas.h.q: What are some research topics in Power Electronics?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__ee__research-areas.h.a: Converters for Renewable Energy and Electric Vehicles, WBG based High gain DC-DC Converters, Design and Development of on board Chargers, Smart Electric Transportation, Grid Ties Solar Inverters

www.iitjammu.ac.in__ee__research-areas.h.q: What are the research areas in Electrical Engineering at IIT Jammu?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__ee__research-areas.h.a: Microelectronics & VLSI Design, Power System, Communication Systems, RF and Microwave, Signal and Image Processing, Power Electronics

www.iitjammu.ac.in__ee__research-areas.h.q: What are some specific research topics under Microelectronics & VLSI Design?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__ee__research-areas.h.a: Compound Semiconductor based emitter and detector, Low Power High Performance circuit designs, Soft error tolerant circuits, VLSI Testing and Hardware Security, Reliability Modeling of Semiconductor Devices

www.iitjammu.ac.in__ee__research-areas.h.q: Which research topics fall under Power System?

www.iitjammu.ac.in__electrical_engineeri.q: What is the purpose of the AADHRIT Lab at IIT Jammu?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__electrical_engineeri.a: The AADHRIT Lab is designed to test and demonstrate the real-time operation of Micro/Smart Grid solutions developed by the research group.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__electrical_engineeri.q: How many high configuration desktops are available in the Low Voltage Lab 1 at IIT Jammu?

www.iitjammu.ac.in__electrical_engineeri.q: What type of equipment is available in the High Voltage Lab at IIT Jammu?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__electrical_engineeri.a: The High Voltage Lab at IIT Jammu is equipped with various high voltage equipment such as transformers, motors, generators, and winding machines.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__electrical_engineeri.q: What is the purpose of the Anechoic Chamber at IIT Jammu?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__electrical_engineeri.a: The Anechoic Chamber at IIT Jammu is a specialized facility established for interference-free measurements of various parameters of antennas and other microwave devices.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__electrical_engineeri.q: What is the focus of research in the IC Reliability, Security & Quality Laboratory at IIT Jammu?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__electrical_engineeri.a: Research in the IC Reliability, Security & Quality Laboratory at IIT Jammu focuses on meeting the challenges in Very Large Scale Integration based design of circuits.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__electrical_engineeri.q: What field is the Prototype Design and Development Lab at IIT Jammu dedicated to?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__electrical_engineeri.a: The Prototype Design and Development Lab at IIT Jammu is dedicated to research in the field of Robotics, IoT, and embedded systems.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__faculty___arunkumarv.a: Dr. Verma earned his master’s and Ph.D. from I.I.T. Delhi, New Delhi, India.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__faculty___arunkumarv.q: Where did Dr. Verma serve as a visiting professor in 2024?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__faculty___arunkumarv.a: Dr. Verma served as a visiting professor at Ontario Tech University, Oshawa, Ontario, Canada, in 2024.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__faculty___arunkumarv.q: Where was Dr. Verma a Postdoctoral research fellow from 2015 to 2016?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__faculty___arunkumarv.a: Dr. Verma was a Postdoctoral research fellow at the Energy Research Institute (ERI@N), Nanyang Technological University (NTU) Singapore, from 2015 to 2016.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__faculty___arunkumarv.q: Where did Dr. Verma work as a visiting graduate researcher from 2014 to 2015?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__faculty___arunkumarv.a: Dr. Verma worked as a visiting graduate researcher at the Smart Grid Energy Research Center (SMERC), University of California, Los Angeles (UCLA), California, USA, from 2014 to 2015.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__faculty___arunkumarv.q: What prestigious fellowship did Dr. Verma win in 2014?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__faculty___arunkumarv.a: Dr. Verma won the prestigious BASE fellowship for advanced solar energy research in 2014.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__faculty___arunkumarv.q: What award did Dr. Verma receive in 2016?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__faculty___arunkumarv.a: Dr. Verma was conferred with the prestigious POSOCO Power System Research Award 2016.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__faculty___arunkumarv.q: What award did Dr. Verma receive in 2023?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__faculty___arunkumarv.a: Dr. Verma was selected for the SERB International Research Experience (SIRE) award in 2023.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__faculty___arunkumarv.q: What editorial roles does Dr. Verma hold?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__faculty___arunkumarv.a: Dr. Verma is an associate Editor for IEEE Transactions on Transportation Electrification, an associate editor for IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Industrial Electronics, an assistant editor of the Smart Grid journal, and a member of the editorial board for the EPE Journal.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__faculty___arunkumarv.q: Where and when did Dr. Verma serve as an instructor for the M.Tech program in Renewable Energy Engineering?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__faculty___arunkumarv.a: Dr. Verma served as an instructor at Defence University College, Ethiopia, and Metal Engineering Corporation, Ethiopia, from 7 May 2016 to 27 May 2016.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__faculty___arunkumarv.q: How many PhD theses has Dr. Verma guided?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__faculty___arunkumarv.a: Dr. Verma has guided 10 PhD theses.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hindi-cell.md.q: What is the purpose of the Hindi Cell at IIT Jammu?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hindi-cell.md.a: The Hindi Cell at IIT Jammu was formed to comply with the Rajbhasha policy and to implement the orders of the Government of India related to Hindi.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hindi-cell.md.q: Who is the chairman of the Rajbhasha Implementation Committee at IIT Jammu?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hindi-cell.md.a: The Director of IIT Jammu is the chairman (ex-officio) of the Rajbhasha Implementation Committee.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hindi-cell.md.q: Who is the Nodal Officer for the Hindi Cell at IIT Jammu?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hindi-cell.md.a: Dr. Shrishail C. is the Member and Nodal Officer (Hindi Cell) at IIT Jammu.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hindi-cell.md.q: What activities does the Hindi Cell organize?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hindi-cell.md.a: The Hindi Cell organizes activities such as Hindi Pakhwada, competitions including essay writing, speech contests, original poetry recitation, and articles, poetry recitations by renowned poets, and the publication of the institute's journal 'Tavyakrrish'.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hindi-cell.md.q: When was the first Hindi Pakhwada organized at IIT Jammu?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hindi-cell.md.a: The first Hindi Pakhwada at IIT Jammu was organized from September 14, 2019, to September 28, 2019.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hindi-cell.md.q: What was the theme of the essay writing competition in 2020?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hindi-cell.md.a: The theme of the essay writing competition in 2020 was 'The positive impact of COVID-19 on student life'.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hindi-cell.md.q: Who were the poets featured in the 2019 Hindi Pakhwada?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hindi-cell.md.a: The poets featured in the 2019 Hindi Pakhwada were Dr. Sanat Tiwari, Dr. Sartaj Ul Hasan, Shri Mahendra Sharma, and Shri Dinesh Raghuvanshi.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hindi-cell.md.q: What is the contact email for the Hindi Cell at IIT Jammu?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hindi-cell.md.a: The contact email for the Hindi Cell at IIT Jammu is hindi [DOT] cell [AT] iitjammu [DOT] ac [DOT] in.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hindi-cell.md.q: What guidelines are provided for submitting articles to the journal 'Tavyakrrish'?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hindi-cell.md.a: Articles should be typed, include the author's full name, position, contact details, and photograph. Political content will not be encouraged, and no remuneration will be given to authors. For assistance with Hindi translation, contact the Hindi Cell.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hindi-cell.md.q: What is the address of the Hindi Cell at IIT Jammu?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hindi-cell.md.a: The address of the Hindi Cell at IIT Jammu is Jagat, NH-44, Nagrota-181221 (Jammu and Kashmir).

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__about-us.html.m.q: What is the focus of the Humanities and Social Sciences Department at IIT Jammu?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__about-us.html.m.a: The focus is on diverse interests ranging from Philosophy to Literatures in English to Economics to Sociology.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__about-us.html.m.q: What unique program does the Department offer?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__about-us.html.m.a: The Department offers a B.Sc. in Behavioural Science and Predictive Analytics.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__about-us.html.m.q: What kind of courses does the Department offer for BTech students?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__about-us.html.m.a: The Department offers core and elective courses for BTech students, inculcating a spirit of curiosity and ethical considerations.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__about-us.html.m.q: What is the goal of the PhD students in the Department?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__about-us.html.m.a: The goal is to broaden the scope of critical enquiry and innovative thinking within the interdisciplinary domains of the humanities and social sciences.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__about-us.html.m.q: What facility has been developed for undergraduate students?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__about-us.html.m.a: An Experiential Language Learning Lab has been developed to aid undergraduate students in honing their Language and Communication skills.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__about-us.html.m.q: How does the Department foster scientific enquiry?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__about-us.html.m.a: The Department fosters scientific enquiry through workshops, conferences, cultural events, programs, and discussions through its collections of books and other reading resources.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__about-us.html.m.q: What kind of environment does the Department aim to uphold?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__about-us.html.m.a: The Department aims to uphold an intellectually stimulating and inclusive environment.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__about-us.html.m.q: What is the larger vision of the Institute according to the Department?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__about-us.html.m.a: The larger vision of the Institute is to enable collaborative interventions that are humanistic, creative, and have a social impact.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__about-us.html.m.q: Who are the members of the Department?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__about-us.html.m.a: The members are dynamic, dedicated, and inquisitive faculty members and scholars.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__about-us.html.m.q: What is the mission of the Department?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__about-us.html.m.a: The mission is to engage with profound questions concerning human existence, psychology, society, economy, culture, and history.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__bachelor-of-sci.q: What is the Bachelor of Science (BS) in Behavioral Sciences and Predictive Analytics program about?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__bachelor-of-sci.a: The program merges Behavioral sciences and predictive analytics, integrating the study of human behavior with advanced data analysis techniques.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__bachelor-of-sci.q: What are the core courses in Year 1 and Year 2 of the BS program?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__bachelor-of-sci.a: Core courses include Behavioral Sciences, Neurosciences, Social Sciences, Humanities, Economics, Mathematics, Computer Programming, Data Science, Machine Learning, Psychology & Cognitive Sciences.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__bachelor-of-sci.q: What are some advanced courses in Year 3 and Year 4 of the BS program?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__bachelor-of-sci.a: Advanced courses include Data Science, Machine Learning, Behavioral Sciences, Business Strategy, Psychology, Economics, Humanities & Social Sciences, and Policy Making Projects.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__faq-main-website.md.a: The Indian Institute of Technology Jammu is located on the north side of Jammu city. The campus is situated on lush green land of Jagti village en-route to Katra via NH-44.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__faq-main-website.md.q: How can one reach IIT Jammu?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__faq-main-website.md.a: You can reach IIT Jammu by air, rail, or road. By Air: IIT Jammu is around 17 km away from Jammu Airport. From Jammu Airport, plenty of auto rickshaws and prepaid taxis are available to reach the IIT Jammu Jagti campus. By Train: Jammu is a railway hub connected to many cities through trains. IIT Jammu is around 13 km away from Jammu Railway Station. From Jammu Railway Station, you can take a bus going to Katra which will drop you in front of the IIT Jammu Jagti Campus or you can take a prepaid taxi or auto rickshaw to reach the campus. By Bus: IIT Jammu is around 12 km away from Jammu Bus stand. From the Bus Stand, you can take a bus going to Katra which will drop you in front of the IIT Jammu Jagti Campus. Alternatively, you can take a matador, three-wheeler, or taxi to reach IIT Jammu Jagti Campus.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__faq-main-website.md.q: What is the climatic condition at IIT Jammu?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__faq-main-website.md.a: The Jammu lies 302m above the sea level. In Jammu, the climate is warm and temperate. According to Köppen and Geiger, this climate is classified as Cwa. Jammu, like the rest of north-western India, features a humid subtropical climate, with summer temperatures reaching 46 °C (115 °F), and temperatures in the winter months occasionally falling below 4 °C (39 °F). June is the hottest month, while January is the coldest month. Please bring a good quality winter jacket/sweater along with gloves and headcap for the winters. You may also bring an umbrella.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__faq-main-website.md.q: Do students need to come to the campus for classwork?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__faq-main-website.md.a: Yes, students need to attend the classwork physically.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__faq-main-website.md.q: Which UG Programs are offered by IIT Jammu for undergraduate students & number of seats in each program?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__faq-main-website.md.a: For the UG programs offered from IIT Jammu & number of seats in each program kindly visit the link https://iitjammu.ac.in/ug-admissions

www.iitjammu.ac.in__faq-main-website.md.q: How many departments are there in IIT Jammu?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__faq-main-website.md.a: There are Eleven departments in IIT Jammu. 1. Department of Bio Sciences and Bio Engineering 2. Department of Chemical Engineering 3. Department of Chemistry. 4. Department of Civil Engineering. 5. Department of Computer Science & Engineering. 6. Department of Electrical Engineering. 7. Department of Humanities and Social Sciences 8. Department of Materials Engineering. 9. Department of Mathematics. 10. Department of Mechanical Engineering. 11. Department of Physics.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__faq-main-website.md.q: What is the UG fee structure at IIT Jammu?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__faq-main-website.md.a: For fee details, visit the link https://iitjammu.ac.in/fee

www.iitjammu.ac.in__faq-main-website.md.q: Does IIT Jammu provide scholarships/financial assistance to the UG Students?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__faq-main-website.md.a: The Institute offers the following scholarships to undergraduate students Institute UG Scholarship Institute Merit-cum-Means Scholarship Institute Free ship Scholarship Institute Free Messing Scholarship Quality for Undergraduates in Engineering, Science and Technology (QUEST) https://iitjammu.ac.in/academic-notifications Ministry of Tribal Affairs (MoTA) Financial Assistance for ST students https://iitjammu.ac.in/academic-notifications Students are encouraged to apply on the National Scholarship Portal (NSP) created by the Government (GoI) of India for students across the country. For detailed information please visit https://scholarships.gov.in/

www.iitjammu.ac.in__faq-main-website.md.q: What are the documents that are required to be submitted for Registration?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__faq-main-website.md.a: List of documents to be submitted by new UG students at the time of the physical reporting & Registration at IIT Jammu: 1. 2 copies of latest passport size photograph 2. Receipt of the payment made to JoSAA at the time of seat acceptance 3. Receipt of online payment of balance fees. For online payment, in place of Entry Number, please write your StudentID 4. Receipt of the online payment of mess charges. For online payment, in place of Entry Number, please write your StudentID 5. Self-attested copy of Aadhar (student) 6. Seat allotment letter (note down JEE-Adv Roll Number and JEE-adv rank) 7. The original 12th mark sheet and certificate and one self-attested copy 8. The original 10th mark sheet and certificate and one self-attested copy 9. The original category certificate (for OBC-NCL / SC / ST as applicable) and one self-attested copy. 10. The original PwD certificate and one self-attested copy. Medical examination will be done by institute medical officer 11. The original EWS certificate in JoSAA format (as produced during seat allotment) 12. Print out of online application form. 13. FORM A - Undertakings of awareness of academic rules at IIT Jammu 14. FORM B & C - Family Income Statement 15. Self-Declaration : Affidavit on Rs.10 Non Judicial stamp paper duly attested by Notary. 16. Form D - Undertakings by the Parents and Student (Offline Mode) 17. FORM E - Hostel declaration form

www.iitjammu.ac.in__faq-main-website.md.q: Do all students need to fill Form B, C and Self Declaration?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__faq-main-website.md.a: If your income category is above 5 lakhs and you are not claiming for any concession/ scholarship from the Institute you need not to fill Form C and Self Declaration. However, Form B is to be filled by students of all income categories.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__hod.html.md.a: To become nationally recognised for excellence in both instruction and research/scholarship.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__integrating-tea.q: Who is heading the project on integrating teaching-learning and digital education?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__integrating-tea.a: Dr Sayantan Mandal is heading the project.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__integrating-tea.q: What is the funding source of the project?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__integrating-tea.a: The project is funded by IIT Jammu.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__integrating-tea.q: What type of study is being conducted?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__integrating-tea.a: The study is a large-scale empirical longitudinal study.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__integrating-tea.q: Which regions are included in the study?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__integrating-tea.a: The study includes institutions from Jammu & Kashmir and Ladakh UTs.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__integrating-tea.q: What approach is used in the study?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__integrating-tea.a: The study employs a mixed-methods approach.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__integrating-tea.q: What is one of the primary aims of the study?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__integrating-tea.a: One of the primary aims is to identify empirically supported strategies for incorporating digital technologies into the classroom.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__integrating-tea.q: What does the study aim to explore?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__integrating-tea.a: The study aims to explore innovative teaching approaches and ensure equitable access to high-quality higher education.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__integrating-tea.q: What are the primary goals of the study?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__integrating-tea.a: The primary goals include familiarizing with current teaching and learning methodologies, examining obstacles, and offering evidence-based recommendations.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__integrating-tea.q: What is the main objective of the project?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__integrating-tea.a: The main objective is to contribute to the growth of IIT Jammu by integrating teaching, learning, and digital technologies for higher education institutions in the northernmost region of India.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__integrating-tea.q: What type of interventions are based on the study?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__integrating-tea.a: The study is based on rigorous research and policy interventions.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__labs.html.md.q: What is the purpose of the Language Experiential Lab at IIT Jammu?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__labs.html.md.a: The purpose of the Language Experiential Lab at IIT Jammu is to reimagine the language learning process through a mobile, non-static experiential learning approach.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__labs.html.md.q: When was the Language Experiential Lab at IIT Jammu established?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__labs.html.md.a: The Language Experiential Lab at IIT Jammu was established in 2025.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__labs.html.md.q: What kind of software systems are used in the Language Experiential Lab?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__labs.html.md.a: The Language Experiential Lab uses dedicated language learning software systems, AI tools, and software.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__labs.html.md.q: What skills does the English language proficiency course aim to develop in students?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__labs.html.md.a: The English language proficiency course aims to develop Listening, Speaking, Reading, and Writing (LSRW) skills in students.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__labs.html.md.q: What kind of environment does the lab provide for students?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__labs.html.md.a: The lab provides a moderated participatory environment with spaces equipped with projecting facilities, AV equipment, and outdoor spaces.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__labs.html.md.q: What certifications do students receive from the Language Experiential Lab?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__labs.html.md.a: Students receive certifications from the lab's software, which are mapped with CEFR guidelines and can be linked with their professional achievements.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__labs.html.md.q: What is the role of the Multipurpose Hall in the lab?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__labs.html.md.a: The Multipurpose Hall is a dedicated space for refining academic English and interpersonal communication skills through activities like Debate, Film, and Reading Clubs.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__labs.html.md.q: What approach does the lab take to cater to the needs of learners?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__labs.html.md.a: The lab caters to the level-based needs of learners by providing an immersive experience with AI tools, software, and interactive activities.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__labs.html.md.q: What is the vision of the Language Experiential Lab?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__labs.html.md.a: The vision of the lab is to ensure that students actively participate and hone their soft skills, setting the foundation for their future career paths.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__labs.html.md.q: Is the language proficiency course a credit course?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__labs.html.md.a: Yes, the language proficiency course offered by the lab is a dedicated credit course.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__locating-femini.q: Who is leading the project at IIT Jammu?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__locating-femini.a: The project is led by Dr. Sukanya Mondal.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__locating-femini.q: What is the funding source of the project?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__locating-femini.a: The project is funded by IIT Jammu.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__locating-femini.q: What is the scheduled completion year of the project?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__locating-femini.a: The project is scheduled to be completed by 2027.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__locating-femini.q: What is the aim of the project?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__locating-femini.a: The aim of the project is to examine the changes which the women's quarter in 19th Century Bengal underwent as an effect of the rapidly evolving public sphere, literacy drive, and various other social-political reformations.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__locating-femini.q: What is the theoretical point of departure for this study?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__locating-femini.a: The theoretical point of departure for this study is Nancy Fraser's critique of Jurgen Habermas' idea of the Public Sphere.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__locating-femini.q: What kind of spaces or situations is the project trying to locate?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__locating-femini.a: The project is trying to locate the spaces or situations when women met to exchange ideas related to contemporary society, politics, etc.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__locating-femini.q: What was the public sphere like in 19th Century Bengal?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__locating-femini.a: The public sphere (public meetings, lectures, etc., even the parlours of well-to-do families) was almost men-only spaces.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__locating-femini.q: What century is the focus of the project?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__locating-femini.a: The project focuses on the 19th Century.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__locating-femini.q: What region is the focus of the project?
What is the specific area of focus within Bengal?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__locating-femini.a: The project focuses on the women's quarter in 19th Century Bengal.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__tribal-project..q: What is the focus of the Tribal Project between IIT Jammu and the Tribal Research Institute?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__tribal-project..a: The focus is on collecting, interpreting, and disseminating Gojri Folklore, understanding the worldview of Gojri through their language, and studying women’s socio-educational status in the life of Gujjars and Bakarwals living in the Pir-Panjal regions of the Union Territory of Jammu and Kashmir.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__tribal-project..q: When was the MOU signed between IIT Jammu and the Tribal Research Institute?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__tribal-project..a: The MOU was signed on 26 June 2022.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__tribal-project..q: Who signed the MOU between IIT Jammu and the Tribal Research Institute?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__tribal-project..a: The MOU was signed in the presence of the Hon’ble Lieutenant Governor, Manoj Sinha.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__tribal-project..q: Who announced the set up of a Tribal Chair at IIT Jammu?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__tribal-project..a: Director, IIT Jammu, Prof Manoj Singh Gaur, announced the set up of a Tribal Chair at the institute.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__tribal-project..q: What are the objectives of the Tribal Chair?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__tribal-project..a: The objectives of the Tribal Chair are to collect, transcreate, digitize, and disseminate the tribal culture of Gujjars and Bakerwals.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__tribal-project..q: Who are the PIs of the Tribal Project?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__tribal-project..a: The PIs of the Tribal Project are: Dr Quleen Kaur Bijral (PI), Dr Amitash Ojha (Co-PI) and Dr Sayantan Mandal (Co-PI).

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__tribal-project..q: Who are the research scholars from the HSS Department involved in the Tribal Project?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__tribal-project..a: Marve and Sayari, research scholars of the HSS Department, have been an integral part of the work.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__tribal-project..q: What are the tangible deliverables of the Tribal Project?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__tribal-project..a: The tangible deliverables of the Tribal Project include digital repositories, reports, websites, and workshops.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__tribal-project..q: What is the primary language focus of the Tribal Project?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__tribal-project..a: The primary language focus of the Tribal Project is Gojri.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__tribal-project..q: What regions of the Union Territory of Jammu and Kashmir are the Gujjars and Bakerwals from?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__hss__tribal-project..a: The Gujjars and Bakerwals are from the Pir-Panjal regions of the Union Territory of Jammu and Kashmir.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__iit-act.md.q: How many Indian Institutes of Technology (IITs) are there in India?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__iit-act.md.a: There are twenty three Indian Institutes of Technology (IITs).

www.iitjammu.ac.in__iit-act.md.q: Which cities have IITs in India?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__iit-act.md.a: IITs are located in Bombay, Delhi, Kanpur, Kharagpur, Madras, Guwahati, Roorkee, Hyderabad, Patna, Bhubaneshwar, Ropar, Jodhpur, Gandhinagar, Indore, Mandi, Varanasi, Tiruppati, Palakkad, Goa, Jammu, Dharwad, and Bhilai.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__iit-act.md.q: What act governs the Indian Institutes of Technology?

www.iitjammu.ac.in__iit-act.md.q: When was The Institutes of Technology Act enacted?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__iit-act.md.a: The Institutes of Technology Act was enacted in 1961.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__iit-act.md.q: Which amendment was made to The Institutes of Technology Act in 1963?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__iit-act.md.a: The Institutes of Technology Act was amended in 1963.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__iit-act.md.q: What is the status of IITs as per The Institutes of Technology Act?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__iit-act.md.a: IITs are declared as 'Institutions of national importance' by The Institutes of Technology Act.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__iit-act.md.q: What does The Institutes of Technology Act lay down?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__iit-act.md.a: The Institutes of Technology Act lays down the powers, duties, and framework for governance of IITs.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__iit-act.md.q: Which government body's website provides further details about The Institutes of Technology Act?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__iit-act.md.a: The Ministry of Human Resource Development (MHRD) website provides further details about The Institutes of Technology Act.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__mathematics__post__m.q: Who is the speaker for the Mathematics colloquium?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__mathematics__post__m.a: Dr. Rajula Srivastava, Asst. Professor, University of Wisconsin-Madison.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__mathematics__post__m.q: What is the title of the Mathematics colloquium?
When is the Mathematics colloquium taking place?
Where is the Mathematics colloquium being held?
What is the abstract of the Mathematics colloquium?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__mathematics__post__m.a: How many rational points with denominator of a given size lie within a specified distance from a compact, “non-degenerate” manifold? A precise answer to this question has significant implications for a host of problems including Diophantine approximation on manifolds. In this talk, I will describe how the analytic and geometric properties of the manifold critically influence this count and provide a heuristic for it. Further, I will talk about recent work which leverages Fourier analytic methods to establish the desired asymptotic for manifolds satisfying a strong curvature condition. At the heart of the proof is a bootstrapping argument that combines Poisson summation, convex duality, and oscillatory integral techniques.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__mathematics__post__m.q: What is the main focus of the talk by Dr. Rajula Srivastava?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__mathematics__post__m.a: Counting rational points with a given denominator size within a specified distance from a compact, “non-degenerate” manifold.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__mathematics__post__m.q: What implications does the precise answer to the colloquium's question have?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__mathematics__post__m.a: Significant implications for a host of problems including Diophantine approximation on manifolds.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__mathematics__post__m.q: What techniques are used in the proof discussed in the colloquium?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__mathematics__post__m.a: Poisson summation, convex duality, and oscillatory integral techniques.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__mathematics__post__m.q: What is the condition for the manifolds discussed in the colloquium?
What is the role of Fourier analytic methods in the recent work discussed?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__mathematics__post__m.a: To establish the desired asymptotic for manifolds satisfying a strong curvature condition.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__mathematics__post__m.q: Who is the speaker for the Mathematics colloquium?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__mathematics__post__m.a: Prof. Emanuel Carneiro, ICTP, Trieste, Italy.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__mathematics__post__m.q: What is the title of the Mathematics colloquium?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__mathematics__post__m.a: Fractional derivatives and the equidistribution of Galois orbits.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__mathematics__post__m.q: What is the abstract of the Mathematics colloquium?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__mathematics__post__m.a: In this talk I will discuss effective versions of the celebrated Bilu's equidistribution theorem for Galois orbits of sequences of points of small height, identifying the qualitative dependence of the convergence in terms of the regularity of the test functions considered. I will present a general Fourier analysis framework that extends previous results in the literature. This is based on a joint work with Mithun Das (ICTP).

www.iitjammu.ac.in__mechanical_engineeri.q: What is the role of mechanical engineers according to the document?

www.iitjammu.ac.in__mechanical_engineeri.q: When was the ICCMDE-2026 conference held?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__mechanical_engineeri.a: The ICCMDE-2026 conference was held from 11–13 February 2026.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__mechanical_engineeri.q: Where was the ICCMDE-2026 conference held?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__mechanical_engineeri.a: The ICCMDE-2026 conference was held at Research and Innovation Park, Indian Institute of Technology Delhi.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__mechanical_engineeri.q: Which departments jointly organized the ICCMDE-2026 conference?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__mechanical_engineeri.a: The ICCMDE-2026 conference was jointly organized by the Department of Mechanical Engineering, Indian Institute of Technology Jammu and the Department of Applied Mechanics, IIT Delhi.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__mechanical_engineeri.q: Who inaugurated the ICCMDE-2026 conference?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__mechanical_engineeri.a: The ICCMDE-2026 conference was inaugurated by Bent F. Sørensen, Naresh Bhatnagar, R. T. Durai Prabhakaran, and Puneet Mahajan.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__mechanical_engineeri.q: Who gave keynote talks at the ICCMDE-2026 conference?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__mechanical_engineeri.a: Keynote talks at the ICCMDE-2026 conference were given by Naresh Bhatnagar (IIT Delhi), Bent F. Sørensen (Technical University of Denmark), Anindya Deb (IISc Bengaluru), and Paul Hazell (University of New South Wales, Australia).

www.iitjammu.ac.in__mechanical_engineeri.q: Who gave plenary talks at the ICCMDE-2026 conference?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__mechanical_engineeri.a: Plenary talks at the ICCMDE-2026 conference were given by Russell J. Varley (RMIT, Melbourne, Australia), Giribaskar (NMIS, UK), Saju Pillai (NIIST, Trivandrum), Raguraman (IIITDM Kancheepuram), Ramadass (DRDO-RDE, Pune), Sanjay Palsule (IIT Roorkee), Inderdeep Singh (IIT Roorkee), Rajnish Sharma (IIT Mandi), Pawan Kumar (CUJ, Jammu), Jayaramulu (IIT Jammu), Badri Prasad Patel (IIT DELHI), S.P.Singh (IIT Delhi), Pradyumna (IIT Delhi), Abhijit Majumdar (IIT Delhi), S Vikrant Karra (IIT Delhi), Naresh Datla (IIT Delhi), and Dr. Gautam Jain, Endolite India Limited, New Delhi.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__mechanical_engineeri.q: What topics were covered in the technical sessions of the ICCMDE-2026 conference?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__mechanical_engineeri.a: The technical sessions covered military-grade composites, biomedical innovations, advanced structural materials, and next-generation manufacturing technologies.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__mechanical_engineeri.q: Who delivered the valedictory session at the ICCMDE-2026 conference?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__mechanical_engineeri.a: The valedictory session at the ICCMDE-2026 conference was delivered by Professor Sawan S. Sinha, Head - Department of Applied Mechanics.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__mechanical_engineeri.q: Where will the selected full-length papers from ICCMDE-2026 be published?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__mechanical_engineeri.a: Selected full-length papers from ICCMDE-2026 will be published in a Special Issue of the Journal of Polymer Composites.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__mechanical_engineeri.q: Which scheme was the ICCMDE-2026 conference organized under?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__mechanical_engineeri.a: The ICCMDE-2026 conference was organized under the Scheme for Promotion of Academic and Research Collaboration (SPARC).

www.iitjammu.ac.in__mechanical_engineeri.q: What are the research areas in the Mechanical Engineering department at IIT Jammu?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__mechanical_engineeri.a: The research areas include Thermal, Design, and Manufacturing. Under Thermal, the areas are Hydrogen Energy and Renewable Energy Systems, Fluid Mechanics, Heat and Mass Transfer, etc. Under Design, the areas are Tribology, Polymer Composites, Design and Manufacturing, etc. Under Manufacturing, the areas are Material Joining, Additive Manufacturing, etc.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__mechanical_engineeri.q: What are some of the specific research topics under the Thermal area?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__mechanical_engineeri.a: Some specific research topics under the Thermal area include Hydrogen Energy and Renewable Energy Systems, Fluid Mechanics, Heat and Mass Transfer, Multi-phase Flow and Heat Transfer, Bio-fluid Mechanics, Gas Turbine Engines and Liquid Rocket Propulsion, Thermal-hydraulics in nuclear reactor and reactor kinetics, Numerical fluid flow and heat transfer, Atmospheric boundary layer fluid dynamics, and HVAC Systems.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__mechanical_engineeri.q: What are the research topics under the Design area?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__mechanical_engineeri.a: The research topics under the Design area include Tribology: Fluid Film Bearings, Mechanical Design, Polymer Composites, Design and Manufacturing, Robotics and Automation, Dynamics and Vibrations, Systems with hysteresis, time delay problems, Computational and Experimental Fracture Mechanics, Fatigue, Phase Field Method, XFEM, Autofrettage.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__mechanical_engineeri.q: What are some of the research topics under the Manufacturing area?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__mechanical_engineeri.a: Some of the research topics under the Manufacturing area include Material Joining, Additive Manufacturing, Artificial Intelligence applications in manufacturing, Machining, Grinding, Sustainable Manufacturing, Surface Texturing, Ultrasonic Profile Finishing, Surface Engineering and Inspection Techniques, Laser Surface Processing, High temperature Coatings, Machining, Micro/Nano Manufacturing, Fabrication of stretchable sensors/devices, Additive Manufacturing of Advanced Materials, Functionally graded and composite Materials, 3D bioprinting, Smart Alloys fabrication, Design for Additive Manufacturing, In-situ monitoring and control of AM Process.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__mechanical_engineeri.q: What is the URL of the Mechanical Engineering department's research areas page?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__mechanical_engineeri.a: The URL of the Mechanical Engineering department's research areas page is https://www.iitjammu.ac.in/mechanical_engineering/research-areas.html.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__mechanical_engineeri.q: What are the research areas under the Thermal category?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__mechanical_engineeri.a: Hydrogen Energy and Renewable Energy Systems, Fluid Mechanics, Heat and Mass Transfer, Multi-phase Flow and Heat Transfer, Bio-fluid Mechanics, Gas Turbine Engines and Liquid Rocket Propulsion, Thermal-hydraulics in nuclear reactor and reactor kinetics, Numerical fluid flow and heat transfer, Atmospheric boundary layer fluid dynamics, HVAC Systems

www.iitjammu.ac.in__mechanical_engineeri.q: What are the research areas under the Design category?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__mechanical_engineeri.a: Tribology: Fluid Film Bearings, Mechanical Design, Polymer Composites, Design and Manufacturing, Robotics and Automation, Dynamics and Vibrations, Systems with hysteresis, time delay problems, Computational and Experimental Fracture Mechanics, Fatigue, Phase Field Method, XFEM, Autofrettage

www.iitjammu.ac.in__mechanical_engineeri.q: What are the research areas under the Manufacturing category?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__mechanical_engineeri.a: Material Joining, Additive Manufacturing, Artificial Intelligence applications in manufacturing, Machining, Grinding, Sustainable Manufacturing, Surface Texturing, Ultrasonic Profile Finishing, Surface Engineering and Inspection Techniques, Laser Surface Processing, High temperature Coatings, Machining, Micro/Nano Manufacturing, Fabrication of stretchable sensors/devices, Additive Manufacturing of Advanced Materials, Functionally graded and composite Materials, 3D bioprinting, Smart Alloys fabrication, Design for Additive Manufacturing, In-situ monitoring and control of AM Process

www.iitjammu.ac.in__mechanical_engineeri.q: What is the specific research topic under the Thermal category that deals with renewable energy systems?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__mechanical_engineeri.a: Hydrogen Energy and Renewable Energy Systems

www.iitjammu.ac.in__physics__research-ar.q: What are the research areas covered by the Materials Engineering department at IIT Jammu?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__physics__research-ar.a: The research areas include Soft matter physics, Energy storage, First-Principles Modelling of materials, Plasma Physics, Solar Energy Harvesting, Nano photonics and Quantum Materials, Quantum Physics, Biophysics, and Optics.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__physics__research-ar.q: What are some of the specific topics under Soft matter physics at IIT Jammu?

www.iitjammu.ac.in__physics__research-ar.q: What are the research topics under Energy storage at IIT Jammu?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__physics__research-ar.a: The research topics under Energy storage include Post Li Ion battery, Metal ion capacitor, Na/Li super ionic conductor, and Solid state battery.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__physics__research-ar.q: What are the research areas under First-Principles Modelling of materials at IIT Jammu?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__physics__research-ar.a: The research areas under First-Principles Modelling of materials include Strongly Correlated materials, Topological electronic materials, Structural and magnetic Transitions, and Optical Properties.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__physics__research-ar.q: What are some of the research topics under Plasma Physics at IIT Jammu?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__physics__research-ar.a: The research topics under Plasma Physics include Strongly Coupled plasma, Dusty Plasma, and Plasma Agriculture.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__physics__research-ar.q: What are the research areas under Solar Energy Harvesting at IIT Jammu?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__physics__research-ar.a: The research areas under Solar Energy Harvesting include Photo Physical Properties and Photo sensing, Solution Processed Solar Cells, and Interface Solar steam Generation.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__physics__research-ar.q: What are some of the research topics under Nano photonics and Quantum Materials at IIT Jammu?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__physics__research-ar.a: The research topics under Nano photonics and Quantum Materials include Plasmonics, Hyperbolic meta-Materials, Optical topological Quantum materials, Non-Linear Optics of Quantum Materials, and Condensed matter Physics.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__physics__research-ar.q: What are the research areas under Quantum Physics at IIT Jammu?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__physics__research-ar.a: The research areas under Quantum Physics include Quantum measurement, Quantum Error corrections, Quantum Control, and Non-Hermitian Physics.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__physics__research-ar.q: What are the research topics under Biophysics at IIT Jammu?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__physics__research-ar.a: The research topics under Biophysics include Non-eq. statistical Mechanics and Computational Biology.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__physics__research-ar.q: What are the research areas under Optics at IIT Jammu?

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__guest-talk-lla.q: Who is giving the guest talk at IIT Jammu?
What is the date of the guest talk?
What is the time of the guest talk?
Where is the guest talk being held?
What is the title of the guest talk?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__guest-talk-lla.a: BATTERY TO SUPERCAPATTERY: An Innovative Energy Storage System

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__guest-talk-lla.q: What is the background of Dr. Ilangovan S. A.?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__guest-talk-lla.a: Dr. Ilangovan S. A. is a Ph.D. from IISc Bangalore, Former Deputy Director (Retd.), VSSC (ISRO) with over 30 years of experience in Energy Storage Systems, cryogenic insulation, thermal protection systems, propellants, polymers, advanced ceramics, and combustion systems.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__guest-talk-lla.q: What is the focus of the abstract of the guest talk?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__guest-talk-lla.a: The focus is on the Supercapattery, an innovative, internally hybridized electrochemical cell that integrates battery and supercapacitor functionalities into a single architecture for efficient energy storage.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__guest-talk-lla.q: What is the significance of the Supercapattery?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__guest-talk-lla.a: The Supercapattery offers a transformative, mass-efficient solution for high-performance space missions and the next generation of electric vehicles by consolidating high-density energy reservoirs and high-rate power delivery into a compact configuration.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__guest-talk-lla.q: What has ISRO developed and qualified?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__guest-talk-lla.a: ISRO has developed and qualified the Supercapattery technology, which has demonstrated high pulse discharge capability with lower internal resistance value.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__guest-talk-lla.q: What is the contact source URL?

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__iit-jammu-sele.q: When was the CBDE program launched?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__iit-jammu-sele.a: The CBDE program was launched on 7th May 2024.
The CBDE program was launched by the Secretary, Department of Higher Education, Ministry of Education, GoI, Sh K Sanjay Murthy.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__iit-jammu-sele.q: What is the purpose of the CBDE program?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__iit-jammu-sele.a: The purpose of the CBDE program is to equip identified Higher Education Institutions (HEIs) and faculty members to focus on Design and Entrepreneurship development in their institutions.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__iit-jammu-sele.q: How many HEIs have been selected for the CBDE program?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__iit-jammu-sele.a: 30 HEIs have been selected for the CBDE program.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__iit-jammu-sele.q: Who is the Nodal Centre for the CBDE program?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__iit-jammu-sele.a: IIITDM Kancheepuram is the Nodal Centre for the CBDE program.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__iit-jammu-sele.q: What is the role of mentors in the CBDE program?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__iit-jammu-sele.a: Mentors from the industry provide stage-wise hand-holding support to students and one-to-one mentoring to faculty.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__iit-jammu-sele.q: What is the aim of the CBDE program according to Smt Debjani Ghosh?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__iit-jammu-sele.a: The aim is to promote generative dialogue among faculty, student teams, and HEI partners to produce actionable outcomes.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__iit-jammu-sele.q: What is the focus of the CBDE program's design?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__iit-jammu-sele.a: The focus is on nourishing students' problem-solving skills and scaling up students' ideas.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__iit-jammu-time.q: What is the duration of the Post Graduate Diploma in Cyber Security at IIT Jammu?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__iit-jammu-time.a: The program is a comprehensive 12-month program.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__iit-jammu-time.q: What are some of the topics covered in the Post Graduate Diploma in Cyber Security?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__iit-jammu-time.a: The program covers topics such as intrusion detection and prevention systems, security protocols for low powered devices, email security, anti-phishing solutions, AI-powered cyber defense, homomorphic encryption applications, infrastructure access management solutions, security solutions for cloud-based virtualized resources, VPNs and proxy development, PKI applications development, and more.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__iit-jammu-time.q: What is the minimum educational qualification required to apply for the program?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__iit-jammu-time.a: Applicants must hold a qualifying degree in B.E, B.Tech, MCA, M.Sc./MS in IT, CS, ECE, EE, or related fields.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__iit-jammu-time.q: Is work experience required to apply for the program?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__iit-jammu-time.a: Yes, a minimum of one year of work experience in IT, CSE, EC, EE, or related fields is required.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__iit-jammu-time.q: What is the minimum percentage or CGPA required in the qualifying degree for eligibility?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__iit-jammu-time.a: A minimum of 55% or 6 CGPA in the qualifying degree is required. Individuals with over 10 years of work experience are eligible with an aggregate of 50% or 5.5 CGPA.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__iit-jammu-time.q: What is the admission process for the program?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__iit-jammu-time.a: Admission to the program is based on relevant academic credentials and a 20-minute virtual interview conducted by an interview panel.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__iit-jammu-time.q: Who can benefit from this program?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__iit-jammu-time.a: This program is ideal for individuals with a background in Software Engineering and IT, as well as those seeking a career shift into the field of cyber security. IT and Engineering professionals aiming to enhance their knowledge in secure technology design and development, system and network security analysis, and application security will greatly benefit from this program.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__iit-jammu-time.a: For more information and to apply for the program, please visit https://bit.ly.3C6zb5K.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__iit-jammu-time.q: What is the main focus of the Post Graduate Diploma in Cyber Security program?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__iit-jammu-time.a: The main focus of the program is to equip individuals with the necessary skills to excel in the field of cyber security through a combination of theoretical knowledge, hands-on learning, and practical projects.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__mathematics-co.q: Who is the speaker for the Mathematics colloquium?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__mathematics-co.a: Dr. Diogo Oliveira e Silva, Instituto Superior Técnico, Lisboa, Portugal.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__mathematics-co.q: What is the title of the Mathematics colloquium?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__mathematics-co.a: Nonlinear Fourier analysis and connections to quantum computing.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__mathematics-co.q: What is the date and time of the Mathematics colloquium?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__mathematics-co.a: 9th October (Thursday), 4-5 pm IST.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__mathematics-co.q: Where will the Mathematics colloquium be held?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__mathematics-co.a: Room 01-AC-2008.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__mathematics-co.q: What is the abstract of the Mathematics colloquium?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__mathematics-co.a: This lecture offers an introduction to the Nonlinear Fourier Transform (NLFT), a powerful analytic tool that extends classical Fourier analysis to nonlinear settings. We present the definition of the SU(2)-valued NLFT and explore its functorial properties with an emphasis on symmetries. We then focus on the nonlinear Plancherel theorem, which serves as a nonlinear analogue for the classical Plancherel identity. We also discuss a deep and elegant equivalence: up to a change of variables, the NLFT coincides with a well-known algorithm in quantum signal processing. Last but not least, we explore the very recent extension of these ideas to the SU(2n)-setting. The lecture assumes familiarity with basic (linear) Fourier analysis but aims to be accessible to a broad audience.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__mathematics-co.q: What is the speaker's affiliation?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__mathematics-co.a: Instituto Superior Técnico, Lisboa, Portugal.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__mathematics-co.q: What is the topic of the lecture?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__mathematics-co.a: Nonlinear Fourier analysis and connections to quantum computing.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__mathematics-co.q: What is the assumed background knowledge for the lecture?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__mathematics-co.a: Familiarity with basic (linear) Fourier analysis.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__mathematics-co.q: What is the recent extension discussed in the lecture?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__mathematics-co.a: The extension of these ideas to the SU(2n)-setting.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__mathematics-co.q: What is the equivalence discussed in the lecture?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__mathematics-co.a: The NLFT coincides with a well-known algorithm in quantum signal processing, up to a change of variables.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__mathematics-co.q: Who is the speaker for the Mathematics colloquium?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__mathematics-co.a: Dr. Nishant Chandgotia, TIFR-Centre for Applicable Mathematics.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__mathematics-co.q: What is the title of the Mathematics colloquium?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__mathematics-co.a: Local algorithms, Conway-Lagarias-Thurston tiling groups and finitely dependent processes.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__mathematics-co.q: When is the Mathematics colloquium being held?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__mathematics-co.a: 30th October (Thursday), 4-5 pm IST.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__mathematics-co.q: Where is the Mathematics colloquium being held?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__mathematics-co.a: Room 01-AC-2008.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__mathematics-co.q: What is the abstract of the Mathematics colloquium?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__mathematics-co.a: Suppose you were given a set of tiles T on the integer lattice Z^2 and wanted to find out whether a given region can be tiled by T. Conway, Lagarias and Thurston paved the way for answering such a question via some tools coming from combinatorial group theory and analysis. On the other hand suppose you were just given some tiles on a sparse but dense set of locations in Z^2 and you wanted to know if T can complete the tiling by looking at what is given locally. The answer to this question comes from local algorithms starting with work by Nathan Linial. Interestingly, these cover complementary cases and are related to a host of questions arising from symbolic and Borel dynamics, probability and combinatorics. We will try to give a gentle introduction to these topics and describe how they relate to finitely dependent processes. This is all in joint work with Aditya Thorat.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__mathematics-co.q: Who is the co-author of the work presented in the colloquium?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__mathematics-co.a: Aditya Thorat

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__mathematics-co.a: https://www.iitjammu.ac.in/post/mathematics-colloquium-on-local-algorithms-conway-lagarias-thurston-tiling-groups-and-finitely-dependent-processes

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__mathematics-co.a: Local algorithms, Conway-Lagarias-Thurston tiling groups and finitely dependent processes

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__samvaad-2026.m.a: Samvaad 2026 was held at Seminar Hall-I, Pushkar Building, IIT Jammu.
Samvaad 2026 brought together faculty members and academic experts from IIT Jammu, IIT Kanpur, and Shri Mata Vaishno Devi University (SMVDU), Katra.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__samvaad-2026.m.q: What was the focus of Samvaad 2026?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__samvaad-2026.m.a: Samvaad 2026 focused on strengthening key aspects of academic practice including classroom communication, student engagement, cognitive aspects of learning, self-learning approaches, and modern pedagogical methodologies.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__samvaad-2026.m.q: Who were the distinguished speakers at Samvaad 2026?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__samvaad-2026.m.a: The distinguished speakers at Samvaad 2026 were from IIT Jammu and IIT Kanpur.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__samvaad-2026.m.q: What type of sessions were conducted at Samvaad 2026?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__samvaad-2026.m.a: Samvaad 2026 featured expert talks, interactive discussions, and demonstration-based sessions.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__samvaad-2026.m.q: What was the objective of Samvaad 2026?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__samvaad-2026.m.a: The objective of Samvaad 2026 was to explore innovative approaches to creating more engaging, reflective, and learner-centric classrooms.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__samvaad-2026.m.q: What does the programme reflect about IIT Jammu?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__samvaad-2026.m.a: The programme reflects IIT Jammu’s continued commitment towards fostering excellence in teaching, faculty development, and academic innovation through collaborative and dialogue-driven learning initiatives.
Samvaad 2026 was organized by the Teaching Learning Unit (TLU) at IIT Jammu.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__samvaad-2026.m.q: What type of learning initiatives does IIT Jammu promote?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__samvaad-2026.m.a: IIT Jammu promotes collaborative and dialogue-driven learning initiatives.

Dr. Atul Banotra and Dr. Sudhakar Modem

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__training-progr.q: When is the 2-Day Training Program on Advanced Manufacturing Technologies at IIT Jammu?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__training-progr.a: The program is scheduled for 27th–28th March 2026.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__training-progr.q: What topics are covered in the training program?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__training-progr.a: The topics covered include CNC Machining and Mastercam-based programming, Wire EDM, Water Jet Machining, advanced CNC operations, Additive Manufacturing (Wire Arc Additive Manufacturing and powder-based methods), and modern Robotic Welding techniques.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__training-progr.q: Who can participate in the training program?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__training-progr.a: The program is open to Polytechnic students, B.Tech/M.Tech students (Mechanical, Production, Mechatronics, Industrial Design), technical staff, and faculty.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__training-progr.q: What are the benefits of participating in the training program?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__training-progr.a: Benefits include a Certificate of Completion from IIT Jammu, training material, workshop access, hi-tea, lunch, and the opportunity to build practical, industry-ready skills.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__training-progr.q: How long is the training program?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__training-progr.a: The training program lasts for 2 days, with 8 hours of sessions each day.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__training-progr.q: What is the mode of the training program?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__training-progr.a: The training program includes both theory and practical sessions.

www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__training-progr.q: What is included in the training material?
www.iitjammu.ac.in__post__training-progr.a: The training material is not explicitly detailed in the provided text.
